title: If you were to shove a small S2 organ into a human
display_title: If you were to shove a small S2 organ into a human
wikitext:
[]: If you were to shove a small S2 organ into a human...             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sat Nov 14, 2009 12:28 am What would be the result?

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sat Nov 14, 2009 12:32 am I'm not sure but im going to guess this:

[]:             Quote Post by Rock-Slash » Sat Nov 14, 2009 12:45 am Depends on which hole you're shoving it in. I have to.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Nov 14, 2009 12:49 am It would be rejected by the body, possibly killing the host.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sat Nov 14, 2009 1:14 am Well lets think about this for a second. Angels are Human and they come with them soooo maybe if we were to put one in it would either work or blow up half of America, thank you 04. Of course that would be "God" see EOE.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Nov 14, 2009 1:38 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Angels are Human and they come with them soooo maybe if we were to put one in it would either work or blow up half of America, thank you 04.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Nov 14, 2009 1:43 am Gendo seems to do okay with one... I suppose there could be some big rejection problems and there's no way to be sure it would do anything at all, but It's just as likely that you'd be able to do ATF voodo if you had one.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Nov 14, 2009 1:53 am You'd get Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Sat Nov 14, 2009 2:32 am AuraTwilight wrote: You'd get Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sat Nov 14, 2009 3:49 am schismatics wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: You'd get Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Archon Eva » Sat Nov 14, 2009 5:15 am I'm going to ignore the shoving and replace it with Surgicly implanted. Apart from having less room in the host's body, i'd say the host would have more energy and strength. He'he, mini eva.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Nov 14, 2009 12:31 pm Considering that they can both generate powerful AT-Fields, it seems likely that Kaworu and Lilith both have the equivilent of S^2 organs somewhere in their bodies. Of note here is that only ADOs seem to naturally possess cores, where their S^2 organ probably resides . Lilith does not seem to have any core, nor do other LDOs, so their S^2 mechanics maybe quite different. Perhaps they don't have one at all? Archon Eva is right about surgical implantation though, of the core at least. We have at least two likely candidates for this. Firstly, Unit-01 as an LDO, has a core implanted in its chest. Secondly, Gendou has the Adam embryo implanted in his hand, which likely contains a small core. Indeed, this core is the probable reason for Gendou's implantation. But neither of these cores contain an S^2 organ, at least initially. Going by the example of Unit-01, before and after its absorption of a core, and comparing the MP Evas to the original series, there does not seem to be a huge difference between cores and bodies with S^2 organs and those without. Unit-01 in fact continues to use electrical power even after its obtains its S^2 organ. So, based off Unit-01, Gendou, Rei and Kaworu, it's likely that the implantation of an S^2 organ in a human would not have any immediate effects, but could only be used if the host body was capable of utilising it.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Nov 14, 2009 1:50 pm Considering that they can both generate powerful AT-Fields, it seems likely that Kaworu and Lilith both have the equivilent of S^2 organs somewhere in their bodies.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Nov 14, 2009 2:27 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Powerful AT Fields have nothing to do with S2 Organs. The Evangelions don't have them and create strong AT Fields just fine, and Rei literally can't have anything even remotely resembling an S2 Organ because she doesn't have the Fruit of Life.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Nov 14, 2009 3:03 pm But they must have some source of energy in order to generate the fields in the first place, else why to the Evangelions use so much electrical power? Remember, even humans have AT Fields; they are simply not able to make them "physical" barriers in the way that Angels can.

[]: Re: If you were to shove a small S2 organ into a human...             Quote Post by Allemann » Sat Nov 14, 2009 3:03 pm CyberXIII wrote: What would be the result?

[]: Re: If you were to shove a small S2 organ into a human...             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Nov 14, 2009 3:13 pm Allemann wrote: CyberXIII wrote: What would be the result?

[]: Re: If you were to shove a small S2 organ into a human...             Quote Post by Allemann » Sat Nov 14, 2009 3:18 pm GasmaskAvenger wrote: so that means vampires & immortals have an S2 organ, right?

[]: Re: If you were to shove a small S2 organ into a human...             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Nov 14, 2009 4:00 pm Allemann wrote: GasmaskAvenger wrote: so that means vampires & immortals have an S2 organ, right?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Nov 14, 2009 4:09 pm AuraTwilight wrote: I know humans have AT Fields. What makes them become physical, however, is the soul being in a PWM body. I speculate that since it's factual that PWM bodies don't need AT Field maintenance (the body remains even without an AT Field), that the subject can then use their "not busy" AT Field to do their cool tricks. Evangelions need so much electrical power to, you know, MOVE. AT Fields are not fueled by physical energy.
